rules.description: Personal data (names, emails, IPs, phone numbers, SSNs, etc.) must never be logged to console, files, monitoring systems, or error tracking services.
rules.fix_guidance: Replace PII with anonymized identifiers or hash values. Use structured logging that redacts sensitive fields.

rules.description: Personal data must be encrypted when stored in databases, files, or any persistent storage.
rules.fix_guidance: Use AES-256 encryption for data at rest. Use encrypted database columns or encrypted file storage.

rules.description: Must obtain explicit, informed user consent before collecting, processing, or storing any personal data. Consent must be freely given, specific, and unambiguous.
rules.fix_guidance: Add a consent checkbox/mechanism. Store consent records with timestamp. Provide consent withdrawal option.

rules.rule: Right to deletion (Right to be Forgotten)
rules.description: Must implement a mechanism for users to request and receive deletion of all their personal data.
rules.fix_guidance: Implement DELETE /user/:id endpoint that removes all user data. Add data retention policies with automatic purging.

rules.description: Only collect personal data that is strictly necessary for the stated purpose. Do not collect SSN, national ID, or biometric data unless essential.
rules.fix_guidance: Remove unnecessary data fields. Document the purpose for each data field collected.

rules.description: Personal data cannot be transferred outside the EU/EEA without adequate protection measures (Standard Contractual Clauses, adequacy decisions, etc.).
rules.article: Art. 46 - Transfers Subject to Appropriate Safeguards
rules.fix_guidance: Use EU-based data centers (eu-west-1, eu-central-1). Implement data residency controls.

rules.description: All database queries must use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection, which could lead to unauthorized access to personal data.
rules.fix_guidance: Use parameterized queries (?, $1, :param). Use an ORM. Never concatenate user input into SQL.

rules.description: API keys, database passwords, and credentials must never be hardcoded in source code where they could be exposed.
rules.fix_guidance: Use environment variables. Use a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault). Add secrets to .gitignore.
